decontamination_notes: Real bug fix from spf13/pflag PR #484, merged 2026-07-02, after model training cutoffs. UintSlice flags parsed each element with strconv.ParseUint(d, 10, 0) (base 10 only), so Go-style base prefixes 0x/0o/0b were rejected even though other pflag integer types accept them. The fix changes the base argument from 10 to 0 (automatic base detection) in the three UintSlice parse sites (Set, fromString, uintSliceConv) in uint_slice.go. Repo sliced at the PR base commit (7475282). pflag has zero external dependencies (stdlib only), so no vendoring is needed and the offline test runs with plain `go test` under --network none. BSD-3-Clause LICENSE preserved. This is a behavioral change (no new symbol), so the package compiles at base; the 3 fail_to_pass tests (hex, mixed hex/octal/binary, large hex) error out at base and pass after the fix, and the pass_to_pass decimal test passes at base and after. Hidden tests use the public FlagSet.UintSlice/GetUintSlice API.
